"CVE-2026-45363", "cvss": {"score": 7.4, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:H/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:N"}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 7.4, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:H/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:N"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-287", "name": "Improper Authentication"}, {"cwe_id": "CWE-326", "name": "Inadequate Encryption Strength"}, {"cwe_id": "CWE-1391", "name": "Use of Weak Credentials"}], "description": "`JWT.decode(token, '', true, algorithm: 'HS256')` accepts an attacker-forged token.
`OpenSSL::HMAC.digest('SHA256', '', payload)` returns a valid digest under an empty key, and no `raise
  InvalidKeyError if key.empty?` precondition exists in the HMAC algorithm.

```
JWT.decode(token, "", true, algorithm: 'HS256')
  -> JWA::Hmac.verify(verification_key: "", ...)
  -> OpenSSL::HMAC.digest('SHA256', "", signing_input) == signature
```

The same path is reached when a keyfinder block or key_finder: argument returns "", nil, or an
array containing nil for an unknown key. JWT::Decode#find_key only rejects literal nil and empty
arrays, and JWT::JWA::Hmac silently coerces nil to "" (signing_key ||= '') before signing.

```
JWT.decode(token, nil, true, algorithms: ['HS256']) { |_h| "" }
  -> find_key returns ""               # "" && !Array("").empty? == true
  -> JWA::Hmac.verify(verification_key: "", ...)
  -> verifies
```
Common application patterns that produce the unsafe value: `redis.get("kid:#{kid}").to_s`, ORM string columns with `default: ''`, `ENV['SECRET'] || '', Hash.new('')` lookups, [primary, fallback] where fallback may be nil. Applications passing a non-empty static key:, or whose keyfinder returns nil / raises on miss, are not affected.

The existing `enforce_hmac_key_length` option would block this but defaults to false. On OpenSSL ≥ 3.5 the empty-key HMAC.digest call no longer raises, so the OpenSSL-3.0 rescue in JWA::Hmac#sign does not fire.

Affects HS256/HS384/HS512 via both JWT.decode (positional key and block keyfinder) and
`JWT::EncodedToken#verify_signature!(key_finder:)`", "epss": {"percentage": 0.00242, "percentile": 0.1523}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-c32j-vqhx-rx3x", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-05-18T17:24:55Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-c32j-vqhx-rx3x", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-c32j-vqhx-rx3x"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-45363"}], "nvd_published_at": null, "published_at": "2026-05-18T17:24:55Z", "references": ["https://github.com/jwt/ruby-jwt/security/advisories/GHSA-c32j-vqhx-rx3x", "https://github.com/jwt/ruby-jwt/commit/db560b769a07bd9724e77ff505011ac01872106f", "https://github.com/jwt/ruby-jwt/releases/tag/v3.2.0", "https://github.com/jwt/ruby-jwt/issues/724", "https://github.com/jwt/ruby-jwt/releases/tag/v2.10.3", "https://github.com/rubysec/ruby-advisory-db/blob/master/gems/jwt/CVE-2026-45363.yml", "https://www.cve.org/CVERecord?id=CVE-2026-45363", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-c32j-vqhx-rx3x"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/jwt/ruby-jwt/security-advisories/GHSA-c32j-vqhx-rx3x", "severity": "high", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/jwt/ruby-jwt", "summary": "ruby-jwt: Empty-key HMAC bypass; cross-language sibling of CVE-2026-44351", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-06-02T22:12:51Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-c32j-vqhx-rx3x", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "3.2.0", "package": {"ecosystem": "rubygems", "name": "jwt"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": ">= 3.0.0, < 3.2.0"}, {"first_patched_version": "2.10.3", "package": {"ecosystem": "rubygems", "name": "jwt"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "< 2.10.3"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: ruby-jwt: Empty-key HMAC bypass; cross-language sibling of CVE-2026-44351
